subject: english
chapter_title: A Homage to Our Brave Soldiers (Bravehearts)

main_sections.title: A Homage to Our Brave Soldiers
main_sections.prose_summary:
This section consists of two letters exchanged between friends Soumya (Bengaluru) and Ananda (Chandigarh), centred on the National War Memorial in New Delhi. In her letter, Soumya describes an educational trip to the memorial — a monument spread over 40 acres near India Gate, constructed between April 2018 and February 2019 and inaugurated by the Prime Minister. She explains that the memorial's 29,000 tablets bear the names of over 26,000 fallen soldiers from post-Independence conflicts including the Indo-China conflict of 1962, Indo-Pak Wars of 1965 and 1971, and the Kargil War of 1999. She describes the memorial's four concentric Chakras: the innermost Amar Chakra (Circle of Immortality) with the eternal flame Amar Jawan Jyoti; the Veerta Chakra (Circle of Bravery) with six bronze battle murals; the Tyag Chakra (Circle of Sacrifice) with granite tablets etched with soldiers' names in gold; and the Raksha Chakra (Circle of Protection) ringed by trees representing soldiers guarding the nation. Soumya shares the citations of two Param Vir Chakra recipients — Lance Naik Albert Ekka (1971 war) and, as her presentation focus, Major Padmapani Acharya (Kargil War 1999, MVC), who quoted the Bhagavad Gita before battle and died after capturing his objective. Soumya closes with deep emotion: the visit brought tears but also a resolve to live worthy of the soldiers' sacrifice.

Ananda's reply is equally moving. She reflects that a war memorial is not just a monument but an expression of deep gratitude for the sovereignty soldiers protect. She recalls her own visit to a war memorial in Chandigarh's Bougainvillea Garden (Sector 3), which prompted her to recall the Hindi poem 'Pushp ki Abhilasha' by Makhanlal Chaturvedi — a flower's longing to be cast on the path of soldiers going to sacrifice for the motherland. Ananda mentions Captain Anuj Nayyar (MVC, Kargil War 1999), whose mother wrote the memoir Tiger of Drass. She also shares the National War Memorial mobile app (available in 21 languages), and ends with her plan to create a collage of inspiring lives with her classmates. Both letters together convey themes of patriotism, gratitude, collective memory, and the duty of citizens to honour soldiers.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Let us read + Let us discuss + Let us think and reflect

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us do these activities before we read. (I) 'Homage' means 'something that is said or done to show respect publicly for someone'. Whom should every Indian pay homage to? Share your answers with your classmates and the teacher. (II) Work in pairs to complete the table by writing any three ways in which we can celebrate the victories and honour the sacrifices of the soldiers. An example has been done for you (visiting a war memorial). Share your answers with your classmates and the teacher.

question_bank.stem: Complete the table: write any three ways in which we can (a) Celebrate the Victories and (b) Honour the Sacrifices of soldiers. (Example given: visiting a war memorial.)
question_bank.table:
Celebrating their Victories|Honouring their Sacrifices
visiting a war memorial|
|
|

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss — Soumya's letter (Section I). Complete the table and answer the questions.

question_bank.stem: Find the paragraph(s) and sentences in the text that evoke each of the following feelings: (1) feeling of wonder (2) heartening (3) sense of motivation (4) pride (5) visually inspiring (6) sadness (7) gratitude (8) inspiration.
Which is India's highest gallantry award?
How would Ananda feel after reading this letter?
Do you think Soumya enjoyed writing this letter? Why or why not?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us discuss — Ananda's letter (Section II). Answer the questions.

question_bank.stem: Why was Ananda reminded of the poem 'Pushp ki Abhilasha'?
How does Soumya's letter help Ananda in pursuing her hobby?

Ananda is inspired to act after reading the letter from Soumya. What actions does she intend to take up? Fill in the table citing the sentences from the text. (1. visit; 2. mobile app; 3. sharing ideas; 4. creating a collage; 5. joining the army; 6. website.)

Action|Sentence from text
visit|
mobile app|
sharing ideas|
creating a collage|
joining the army|

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — Section I. Read the extracts given below and answer the questions. (Extract 1: Soumya's description of the Amar Jawan Jyoti and obelisk. Extract 2: Ananda's view of the war memorial as expression of deep gratitude.)

question_bank.stem: Complete the analogy with a word from the extract: flame: valour :: wreath: ______________
How does the lighting design make the monument visually appealing?
State whether the following sentence is a fact or an opinion: 'It truly is a majestic site…'
What does the everlasting flame suggest?
The tone of the writer in this extract is ______________. A. modest B. light-hearted C. admiring D. nostalgic
Choose a line from the extract which shows that Ananda was aware about the National War Memorial.
What might be the most likely reason for people to consider a war memorial as a monument?
Fill in the blank with a suitable word from the extract: 'We conduct special competitions at school to ______________ our Independence day.'
Select a phrase from the extract showing that Ananda is expressing an opinion.
Complete the sentence with a suitable reason: Ananda refers to the sacrifice of soldiers as 'supreme' sacrifice because ______________.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Let us think and reflect — Section II. Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: What is the significance of the National War Memorial?
Lance Naik Albert Ekka is an example of selflessness, determination, and exceptional courage. Elaborate.
Explain how the National War Memorial and Museum app will be helpful in spreading awareness about war heroes.
Why is it our duty to pay homage to our brave soldiers?
What is the main idea of this text? How does it justify the title, 'A Homage to our Brave Soldiers'?
Why were the students awestruck and humbled? Explain in your own words.

listening.transcript_text: GUNJAN's SPEECH (Armed Forces): Good morning, respected teachers and my dear friends. Today, I, Gunjan, of Grade 7-C, stand before you to share why I dream of joining the Indian Armed Forces when I grow up. Serving our country is the most honourable job one can have. It means protecting and securing our nation, even if it means risking one's life. The thought of wearing the uniform, which can only be earned through hard work, fills me with pride and a strong desire to serve India. The Armed Forces teach you to live a disciplined and organised life. This keeps both the mind and body active. Soldiers are always ready to face challenges because of this discipline. Additionally, regular exercise and training keep soldiers physically fit and ready for any situation. This preparation is something I greatly admire and wish to achieve. Lastly, being a soldier means having new experiences and adventures everyday. Each day brings new challenges, teaching important lessons, and helping one grow. The Armed Forces offer a life filled with honour, self-respect, safety, and security for both the soldier and their families. It is a life that teaches loyalty, duty, service, and honesty. This is why I want to join the Indian Armed Forces. Thank you.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a girl speak about why she wishes to join the Armed Forces when she grows up. As you listen, answer the questions given below in two–three exact words you hear.

question_bank.stem: What type of job does Gunjan think the Armed Forces is?
What feature of the Armed Forces fills Gunjan with pride?
How does the disciplined life of the Armed Forces impact the body and mind, according to Gunjan?
List any one aspect about a job in the Armed Forces that Gunjan feels helps someone grow.
State any two things that Gunjan feels life in the Armed Forces teaches.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Work in pairs and identify the different professions displayed in the picture. Imagine that you dream of or wish to pursue some of these professions and vocations. Take turns to express this along with a reason. Do not forget to include what preparation would be required to fulfill the dream. Speak about all the given professions and vocations.

Study the words: disregard, inconsequential, immortal, undaunted. These words are made by adding prefixes (dis-, in-, im-, un-) to root words. Match the prefixes with their meanings and root words to create new words. Then complete the paragraph using the new words created.

question_bank.stem: Match prefixes with their meanings and root words to create new words: (re- / badly,wrongly / questionable), (mal- / beyond,outside / activate), (un- / opposed to;against / define), (anti- / former functioning / service), (extra- / lacking;not / ordinary), (ex- / remove / social), (de- / do again). Example: extra- + ordinary = extraordinary.
question_bank.table:
Prefix|Meaning|Root Word|New Word
re-|do again|activate|reactivate
mal-|badly, wrongly|service|malservice
un-|lacking; not|questionable|unquestionable
anti-|opposed to; against|social|antisocial
extra-|beyond, outside|ordinary|extraordinary
ex-|former functioning|define|ex
de-|remove|define|dedefine

question_bank.stem: Complete the paragraph using the new words created: 'Soldiers undergo (i) extraordinary training to prepare for their challenging duties. They learn to (ii) ____________ explosives and handle (iii) ____________ equipment with precision. They stand as a powerful force against (iv) ____________ elements, displaying (v) ____________ bravery. (vi)________ members often share their experiences to inspire new recruits. Their service can continually (vii) ____________ the essence of commitment and sacrifice.'

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Find words associated with 'brave' by filling in the missing vowels in the boxes given below: V_L__NT, C__R_G_, H_R__C, V_L__R, G_LL_NT, F__RL_SS, D_R_NG, B_LD, _ND__NT_D.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the missing vowels to find words associated with 'brave': V_L__NT, C__R_G_, H_R__C, V_L__R, G_LL_NT, F__RL_SS, D_R_NG, B_LD, _ND__NT_D.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Fill in the blanks with the correct opposite words of 'brave' from the box (fear, cowardly, timid, afraid) to complete the following sentences.

question_bank.stem: Fill in the blanks with the correct opposite of 'brave': (1) Ajay's ____________ refusal to stand up for his friend was disappointing to everyone. (2) The ____________ child hid behind his mother, too scared to meet the new teacher. (3) Anjali's ____________ of heights prevented her from climbing the tall ladder. (4) Sushma felt ____________ as she entered the dark, abandoned building alone. [Box: fear, cowardly, timid, afraid]

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Read the following sentences from the text and divide them into main clause and subordinate clause. An example has been done (I aspire to be courageous like him | when I join the army!). Then study the grammar of subordinating conjunctions (when, because, though, so that).

question_bank.stem: Divide these sentences into main clause (Part 1) and subordinate clause (Part 2): (1) I aspire to be courageous like him when I join the army! (2) They exchange letters and share their feelings of gratitude for the freedom they enjoy because of the sacrifices made by the bravehearts of the country. (3) Though seriously wounded in this encounter, he continued to fight alongside his comrades. (4) The sacrifices of the soldiers make monuments like the National War Memorial extremely important so that we should never forget the cost of freedom.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Combine the following pairs of sentences using suitable subordinating conjunctions from the box (because, unless, when, where, though). Then complete the following sentences with suitable main clause or subordinate clause.

question_bank.stem: Combine using subordinating conjunctions (because, unless, when, where, though): (1) Wasi burst into tears. He heard the good news. (2) Himani was very nervous. She delivered a great presentation. (3) This is the place. I was born. (4) The students performed very well. They studied diligently. (5) Water the plants. They will die.

Complete with a suitable main clause or subordinate clause: (1) Farheen was late to work because ________________. (2) The dog started barking when ________________. (3) She completed the marathon although ________________. (4) ________________ while they were hiking in the mountains. (5) Abhishek received a promotion after ________________. (6) ________________ before the deadline approached. (7) We waited in the car until ________________. (8) ________________ as the waves rushed to the shore.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You have read about the sacrifices made by the Armed Forces during war. However, the Armed Forces also play a key role during peace time. Write a letter to your friend sharing how the Armed Forces contribute their services during the times of natural calamities (floods, earthquakes, etc.), infrastructure development (building bridges in remote areas, etc.) and for welfare initiatives (medical camps, establishment of libraries, etc.). Use the prompts given below.

Every branch of the Armed Forces has a motto. Read the mottos: (1) Indian Army — Sevā Paramo Dharmaḥ (Service Before Self); (2) Indian Air Force — Nabhaḥ Spr̥śaṁ Dīptam (Touch the Sky with Glory); (3) Indian Navy — Sham No Varunah (Be Auspicious Unto Us Oh Varuna). India has many other Forces that serve the nation (e.g. Central Reserve Police Force — CRPF). Find out about such Forces and discuss with the teacher.
Create a collage capturing the lives and stories of people that inspire us. In groups of four, prepare a collage on a chart paper on any such person and present it in the class.

question_bank.stem: In groups of four, create a collage on a chart paper about a person who inspires you and present it in class.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Many women have joined the Armed Forces and are serving the nation. Have a discussion in the class with examples.
